title: Acronia cassidata
taxonomy: Acronia cassidata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family is one of the largest and most diverse in the plant kingdom, comprising over 13,000 species. Within the Rubiaceae family, Acronia cassidata is placed in the genus Acronia, which is native to Southeast Asia. The genus Acronia is also part of the tribe Psychotrieae, which comprises many species of flowering plants. Further classification of Acronia cassidata is also possible at the level of the order Gentianales, which includes many flowering plant families.

intro: Acronia cassidata, also known as the Acronia tree, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. The Acronia cassidata is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of fever and cough. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acronia cassidata is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules and tablets to support overall well-being.
